seoTitle: Picton vs Picton East — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Picton and Picton East. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Picton vs Picton East: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Picton and Picton East.
heading: Picton vs Picton East — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Picton, Western Australia and Picton East, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Picton East has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Picton (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Picton or Picton East safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Picton has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Picton East scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Picton scores 56/100 for bushfire risk. Picton East scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Picton or Picton East?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Picton East has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
